directive.title: Dashboard performance budget assertion and instrumentation

auditor.summary: The build implements most of the performance instrumentation requirements correctly, including Server-Timing headers, health endpoint with degradation detection, fire-and-forget audit logging, and client-side timing hooks. However, there are two CRITICAL/HIGH issues: (1) The dashboard page imports a 'use client' hook directly in a server component which will cause a Next.js build error, and (2) the audit log entity_type uses 'PERF_TELEMETRY' instead of the required 'PERFORMANCE_METRIC'. Additionally, the test mock structure may not properly match the route handler implementation. The build is recoverable with these fixes but cannot pass in its current state.

top_issues.description: Server component imports a 'use client' hook directly. Next.js 14 requires 'use client' hooks to be imported only from client components or wrapped in a client boundary. This will cause a build error.

auditPerfLog stores entity_type as 'PERF_TELEMETRY' but acceptance criterion 21 requires event_type 'PERFORMANCE_METRIC'. The audit_logs table schema shows entity_type is a text field, but the criterion specifically requires queryable by event_type='PERFORMANCE_METRIC'.

Test mock structure may not match actual route handler implementation. The mock returns data via .limit() but the actual GET handler in app/api/rfq/route.ts calls .limit(100) on the query chain. The mock needs to properly chain all methods used by the handler.

gate.reason: compile gate: 2 unresolved import(s) after 2 attempt(s)
